models: Claude CLI attempted; bounded call timed out
Grok CLI skipped after external-model wrapper timeout

barriers.title: Fresh score still disagrees with the status snapshot
barriers.detail: The score-only refresh wrote recall_metrics/nightly_20260708_124724 with unique variants 2589/3010 (86.0%), rows 5517/6833 (80.7%), and PMIDs 1273/1502 (84.8%). docs/RECALL_STATUS.md still reports 2591 unique, 5518 rows, and 1274 PMIDs from the June 12 status snapshot, so the latest matcher/scorer changes need reconciliation before changing the authoritative status.

barriers.title: Counted precision and count semantics are now first-class blockers
barriers.detail: The fresh failure split is led by pipeline_only_extras (481) and count_semantics (326), ahead of source_missing_or_stub (287) and source_abstract_only (102). Treat these as precision/count-attribution work, not generic extraction recall work.

barriers.title: SCN5A, RYR2, and KCNH2 still hold the 90% gap
barriers.detail: KCNQ1 unique-variant recall remains over target at 563/622 (90.5%), while SCN5A is 1020/1183 (86.2%), RYR2 is 565/675 (83.7%), and KCNH2 is 441/530 (83.2%). Aggregate gaps remain +120 unique variants, +633 rows, and +947 patients/carriers to 90%.

barriers.title: Unaffected/control attribution remains the MAE risk
barriers.detail: Carriers MAE is stable at 0.615 and affected MAE at 0.494, but unaffected MAE is still 1.192. Any source or extraction gain should be gated against control/unaffected count inflation, especially in regex_table and cohort-table rows.

barriers.title: High-yield missing PMIDs are concentrated enough for bounded experiments
barriers.detail: Fresh missing-row leaders include KCNQ1 17192539 (56), KCNQ1 23631430 (36), SCN5A 15840476 (31), SCN5A 20541041 (26), RYR2 19398665 (25), RYR2 27452199 (23), KCNH2 29650123 (20), and KCNH2 16922724 (19). Use these as small acceptance-gated tests, not a broad live rerun.

ideas.title: Diff the accepted June status against the fresh scorer output
ideas.detail: Compare recall_metrics/linearized_tables_20260612_strict or codex_verify_20260612 against nightly_20260708_124724 for PMIDs, rows, unique variants, patients, affected, unaffected, counted precision, and MAE. Acceptance gate: explain the -2 unique/-1 row/-1 PMID delta before updating docs/RECALL_STATUS.md.

ideas.title: Classify pipeline_only_extras before more acquisition
ideas.detail: Split the 481 pipeline_only_extras into zero-count linkage rows, count-bearing carrier rows, wrong-gene/annotation-table rows, and matcher-denominator artifacts. Acceptance gate: reduce counted extras or prove most extras are harmless zero-count linkage.

ideas.title: Run a count-semantics pass on regex_table/control rows
ideas.detail: Preserve raw count columns and classify per-variant carriers versus study-wide, affected/proband/case, unaffected/asymptomatic/control, and population counts before DB writes. Acceptance gate: unaffected MAE holds or improves while precision_vs_counted_gold_pmids stays at or above 77.2%.

ideas.title: Target the top missing-PMID set with source-aware refresh
ideas.detail: Regenerate a small worklist from the fresh missing reports, then run source QC and targeted refresh only for the highest-yield PMIDs with usable or recoverable table bodies. Acceptance gate: aggregate unique variants or rows move without MAE or counted-precision regression.

ideas.title: Extend matcher lanes only where extracted evidence already exists
ideas.detail: Use the unmatched/missing reports to isolate indels, compound rows, splice/IVS records, and structural/CNV forms that are already in SQLite but fail exact matching. Acceptance gate: recovered matches increase unique-variant recall without adding counted extras.

solutions.title: Add a previous-artifact delta block to the dashboard workflow
solutions.detail: Have the nightly job compute a compact delta versus the latest accepted summary so small regressions in recall, MAE, and counted precision are visible before the morning readout.

solutions.title: Use counted precision as a promotion gate
solutions.detail: Do not accept source, extraction, or matcher changes unless precision_vs_counted_gold_pmids holds at or above the current 77.2% floor, or improves alongside recall.

solutions.title: Keep source acquisition and count cleanup separate
solutions.detail: Source/table acquisition should target missing/stub/abstract-only and missing-table-body failures; count-semantics work should target count-bearing extras and MAE. Mixing them will hide whether a change improved recall or just shifted errors.

solutions.title: Treat full-coverage enrichment as opt-in until scored
solutions.detail: The architecture now has full-coverage walk-to-taper, carrier guard, and variantFeatures enrichment hooks. Keep them behind explicit flags and require score-only evidence before promoting their outputs into canonical recall claims.

solutions.title: Keep KCNE1 out of recall headlines
solutions.detail: KCNE1 remains extraction-only until a normalized gold input exists. It can appear as no-gold context in the dashboard but should not affect scored recall claims.

model_notes.Claude CLI: Installed, but the bounded noninteractive critique wrapper did not return and was interrupted. No fresh Claude critique was used for this run.
model_notes.Grok CLI: Installed, but skipped after the external-model wrapper timeout to avoid blocking the dashboard. No fresh Grok critique was used for this run.
model_notes.Run note: The previous dashboard sidecar contained July 7 Claude/Grok critiques; this run replaces them with an explicit timeout note rather than reusing stale second opinions.
model_notes.Publish note: Dashboard commit/push is blocked locally because the generated pre-commit hook invokes .venv/bin/python -m pre_commit, but this .venv has neither pre_commit nor pip. Hooks were not bypassed.
